steps.title: Find the sphere volume
steps.content: The volume of a sphere is $V=\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$. With radius $4$, the volume is $V=\frac{4}{3}\pi(4)^3=\frac{256\pi}{3}$ cubic inches.

steps.title: Multiply by the density
steps.content: Mass equals density times volume. Use the density $48$ ounces per cubic inch: $48(\frac{256\pi}{3})=16(256\pi)=4{,}096\pi$ ounces.

steps.title: Match the mass to choices
steps.content: The mass is $4{,}096\pi$ ounces, which is the same as $\frac{4{,}096\pi}{1}$. The answer is D.
